architecture_fit.note: base_model.py minus MLP — one self-attention block, attention-only, minimal delta; saturates achievable orthogonality (0.83, equal to the hand-built ideal ceiling).

baseline_comparison.note: Three comparators under the same metric: untrained strawman (0.63), linear baseline (0.50), hand-built ideal (0.83) — the claim reads as a clear gap, not a lone number.

faithfulness.note: Clean causal ablation: uniform-weight attention collapses factor reconstruction 0.90→0.49 (chance), though the link to query orthogonality specifically is one step indirect.

operating_range.note: Noise sweep 0.0→1.0 (robustness 0.93); orthogonality holds ~0.83 until degrading at 1.0, alignment degrades earlier (1.0→0.65), showing where it breaks.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Includes a hand-built ideal encoder (token-decode → q = factors @ fdir) reproducing the target as an upper bound, demonstrating the mechanism by hand.

visual_judgement.note: Demo leads with a 4-way orthogonality bar chart (the gap), plus faithfulness bar, cosine heatmap, noise line and per-factor separation — each panel maps to a rubric item, all data-driven.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies every panel against the goal: gap vs comparators, the single flip-test, the literal metric definition (heatmap), operating range, and alignment histograms.

notes: A trained single attention layer (base_model.py minus MLP) on a diagonal-masked retrieval task makes orthogonal factor subspaces emerge, saturating the achievable orthogonality (0.83, matching the hand-built ideal) far above the linear baseline (0.50). Strong baseline panel, a clean ablation collapsing reconstruction to chance, a well-covered noise sweep, and a hand-built upper bound. Mean of scored items ≈4.6 → good.
